name: Build a Chrome Extension
description: A Chrome browser extension using Manifest V3

universal.note: These are installed during onboarding, not per-blueprint

blueprint.name: Google Chrome
blueprint.blockerMessage: Chrome extensions need Google Chrome installed to test. Download it from google.com/chrome.

blockers.name: Google Chrome
blockers.reason: Cannot auto-install — user must download from google.com/chrome
blockers.resolution: Install Google Chrome, then relaunch TWEEBS

authRequirements.name: Chrome Web Store Developer Account
authRequirements.note: Only needed for publishing. Development and testing work without it. One-time $5 registration fee.

mcpConfigs.name: chrome-devtools
mcpConfigs.userNotice: Chrome will open in the background so the frontend engineer can test the extension. You'll see it load automatically — no action needed.

postScaffold: Create manifest.json with Manifest V3 structure
Set up extension entry points: popup, background service worker, content scripts

initialContext: The user wants to build a Chrome extension. Ask what it does in one sentence and which part of the browser it interacts with — does it add a popup, modify pages the user visits, add a sidebar, or work in the background? This determines the extension's architecture (popup, content script, background service worker, side panel, or combination). Then hand off to architect for extension architecture, designer for popup/UI design, and frontend engineer for implementation.

popup.description: Small UI that opens when user clicks the extension icon in the toolbar
popup.useWhen: Extension needs a quick settings panel, dashboard, or action trigger

contentScript.description: JavaScript that runs in the context of web pages the user visits
contentScript.useWhen: Extension needs to read or modify web pages (highlight text, add buttons, scrape data)

backgroundServiceWorker.description: Persistent background script that handles events and manages extension state
backgroundServiceWorker.useWhen: Extension needs to handle alarms, intercept network requests, manage cross-tab state

sidePanel.description: Panel that opens alongside the page content
sidePanel.useWhen: Extension needs a persistent, larger UI alongside the page

optionsPage.description: Full settings page for the extension

permissions.note: The architect Tweeb determines which Chrome permissions are needed based on the extension's functionality and includes only the minimum required set in manifest.json

loadUnpacked: Build the extension: npm run build
Click 'Load unpacked' and select the dist/ folder
testingStrategy.automation: Chrome DevTools MCP can inspect the popup and content script output. The frontend engineer Tweeb loads the extension, tests it, and iterates.
testingStrategy.hotReload: CRXJS Vite plugin supports HMR — changes to the extension code hot-reload in Chrome during development
